key_points: Only two housing options rated good: The Gardens and Cherry Street
Market is split: luxury-priced new or cheap-but-bad old, nothing in between
Off-campus common; bike recommended for commuting from apartments
key_quotes: College Station has a massive issue with convenient housing; either you will find a new luxurious option with high prices or old and relatively cheap, you cannot find something in between
The Gardens TAMU & Cherry Street apt everything else is bad
What is the best bike to get for TAMU the college is quite big and my apartment is off campus

key_points: Cult-like identity is the dominant social force on campus
Legacy families drive homogeneous social scene; many students know same people from high school
Some students find it hard to meet new people outside their existing friend group
key_quotes: Had multiple family members and people I graduated with go to A&M and they've all agreed it's a cult
Many students don't know how to have a conversation or meet new people unless it's only their group of friends they know
I don't even want to go there because it's all the same people from hs

key_points: No substantive student feedback on dining quality in the data
Halal food availability raised as concern by prospective students
Campus dining not a notable talking point positively or negatively
key_quotes: Am I going to have trouble with getting Halal food in the dining halls?
I remember the year before going to TAMU there was a story of some kids from my hometown driving home and getting into a head on crash

key_points: A student died by suicide during the data period, noted without institutional response
Political turmoil and firings causing documented stress and campus division
Corps of Cadets cited as causing severe sleep deprivation and mental exhaustion
key_quotes: Texas A&M student Brianna Aguilera took her own life in fall near UT
5 hours of sleep daily, constant surveillance by my superiors...physical exhaustion, demotivation
I know there is a huge rift on campus... The end goal has always been to divide people

key_points: 4.0 GPA students reporting zero academic scholarship offers
International students face high tuition with broken scholarship links on the website
Strong full-ride STEM scholarship exists but highly competitive with strict GPA requirements
key_quotes: I have 4.0 unweighted gpa, 5.2 weighted, 1360 SAT, bunch of volunteer hours, but I did not get any academic scholarship for TAMU
I just saw that Texas A&M has a $90 application fee and also has high tuition for international students... their link for scholarships was broken for me
They require you to maintain a 3.5 GPA, do a study abroad program, an internship, and a fifth year MS Business degree

key_points: Professors actively being instructed to remove controversial topics from curriculum
ETAM system forces general engineering first year with GPA barriers to declare major
Changing majors requires meeting grade thresholds, frustrating students who want flexibility
key_quotes: Yesterday I had not one but two professors mention that they're no longer able to say 'climate change' and are instructed to now only say 'extreme weather events'
AND IF U WANT TO CHANGE MAJORS YOU HAVE TO SOMETIMES MAKE AS AND BS IN CERTAIN CLASSES TO CHANGE WHICH IS RIDICULOUS
Professors are being instructed to remove certain controversial topics from the curriculum, and are being directed not to speak about them

key_points: Two university presidents fired in two years over political pressure from state legislature
Chancellor donated to SPLC-designated extremist group; uses university resources for personal communications
Politicians, not educators, are functionally deciding what gets taught in classrooms
key_quotes: Two fucking embarrassing university president firings in just over 2 years. TAMU is a laughing stock
Texas A&M Chancellor Glenn Hegar donated $2750 to the True Texas Project, a group that believes there is a war on white America
At Texas A&M, politicians, not professors, decide what is taught in classrooms. This is A&M's reputation now

key_points: Campus is large and architecturally striking but described as bland and colorless
College Station is extremely college-centric with limited life outside the university bubble
3 hours from Dallas, 1-2 hours from Houston and Austin; isolated location is a real factor
key_quotes: Everything was bland and the walks were atrocious. Not a lick of soul on this campus
Campus is 1 to 2 hours from Houston and Austin. 3 hours to Dallas... Could use some color though
I long for the days when all the college kids are gone. So much better when the roads are empty

key_points: Aggie alumni network is genuinely powerful and well-documented, 'Aggies hire Aggies' is real
Fortune 100 has TAMU tied for most CEOs with Penn and Boston College
Academic freedom crisis is now a hiring concern; tech executives publicly saying they won't hire from schools banning Plato
key_quotes: I have been hiring for 20 years. In tech. I wouldn't hire anyone from a school that bans Plato
Guy I knew started a business with the term 'Aggieland' in the name and got business solely on that from alumni
Fortune 100 has TAMU and Penn tied for the pack, each with 4 CEOs

key_points: In-state tuition competitive but engineering-track students risk extra semester if ETAM GPA missed
Out-of-state costs run $60k+/year with limited scholarship support for internationals
Degree value being actively questioned as academic reputation declines under political interference
key_quotes: Feel sorry for all the good kids who go here who are having their degree devalued by culture war turds
As someone who doesn't go to A&M it's been crazy to watch this elite, globally respected institution speedrun the death of its reputation
TAMU Blinn will most likely cost me around 100k in student debt

key_points: Football and volleyball wins generate massive communal pride but are masking serious institutional crisis
Deep split between traditional Aggie identity and students horrified by political direction of school
Trans and LGBTQ+ students report feeling genuinely unsafe; straight conservative students mostly unbothered
key_quotes: I am a trans student at A&M. I am not your political scapegoat
To be fair. A&M radicalized me. Now I care about people
Lotta stuff happening to churn the news cycle away from Welsh, yeah. Go football tho

key_points: **Academic Freedom:** Professors instructed to remove topics; 'climate change' banned by name
**Administration:** Grad student held in ICE detention with university refusing to pay legal fees or comment
**Campus Safety:** Bryan PD documented profiling students; ICE activity emails sent to grad departments
key_quotes: Reminder: Graduate student Will Taeheung Kim is still in ICE detention and Texas A&M has made no comment and refuses to pay his legal fees or stand up for him, while taking his tuition money
Fuck the Bryan Police Department. Bunch of losers trying to make something of nothing to prey on young students
Newly hired professor at A&M Philosophy withdraws from appointment because of lack of academic freedom at university

key_points: Volleyball program just won national championship sweeping every #1 seed — world-class athletics beyond football
Aggie Ring tradition creates lifelong alumni loyalty and genuine career networking unlike most schools
Academic Plaza protests show a real, active student community pushing back on institutional failures
key_quotes: BEAT THE #1 OVERALL IN THEIR HOUSE, SWEEP THE OTHER #1 IN THE SEMIS, SWEEP THE LAST #1 IN THE NATTY
I'm a very left-leaning POC. I worked hard for my ring... I wear it as a reminder of the time, work, the ups and downs it took to get it
I was surprised to find there were quite a few conservatives participating at the academic freedom rally tonight
